questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If the units of mass, length and time are doubled then unit of angular momentum will be
questions.solution:
The dimensions of angular momentum are

               J=ML2T-1

When units of mass, length and time are doubled, than

             J′=2M2L22T-1

⇒        J′=4ML2T-1=4 J

Unit of angular momentum is quadrupled.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
options: the dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> are the same
the dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> are the same
questions.solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mrow><mi>A</mi><mtext>sin</mtext><mo>⁡</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi><mtext>cos</mtext><mo>⁡</mo><mi>θ</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>A</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>B</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>; Dimensions of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>B</mi></math> would be same as two quantities can be added only when they have same dimensions. So from given equation, we can say that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Z</mi></math> is dimensionless.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Suppose a quantity can be dimensionally represented in terms <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math>, that is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="]" open="["><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">M</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></msup><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></msup></mrow></mfenced></math>. The quantity mass
options: maybe represented in terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math> if <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>

maybe represented in terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>y</mi></math> if <math> <mrow> <mtext>a</mtext> <mo>≠</mo> <mn>0</mn> </mrow> </math>

Can always be dimensionally represented in terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></math>

Can never be dimensionally represented in terms of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">L</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">T</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi></math>
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: The dimension of stopping potential <math><msub><mrow><mi>V</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>0</mn></mrow></msub></math> in photoelectric effect in units of Planck’s constant ‘ <math><mi>h</mi></math> ’, speed of light ‘ <math><mi>c</mi></math> ’ and Gravitational constant ‘ <math><mi>G</mi></math> ’ and ampere <math><mi>A</mi></math> is:
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
The ratio of the dimensions of Planck constant and that of moment of inertia has the
dimensions of
questions.solution:
Dimensions of Planck's constant $=M L^{2} T^{-1}$
Dimensions of moment of inertia $=M L^{2}$
Ratio of dimensions of Planck's constant and moment of inertia is $\frac{[h]}{[I]}=\frac{M L^{2} T^{-1}}{M L^{2}}=T^{-1}$
Dimension of frequency $=T^{-1}$
Dimension of velocity $=L T^{-1}$
Dimension of angular momentum $=M L^{2} T^{-1}$
Dimension of time $=T$
Thus, ratio of dimensions of Planck's constant and that of moment of inertia has the dimensions of frequency
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $C$ be the capacitance and $V$ be the electric potential, then the dimensional formula of $\mathrm{CV}^2$ is
questions.solution:
We know that energy, $E=\frac{1}{2} C V^2$
Dimensions of $C V^2=$ Dimensions of energy, $E$
$=\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^0\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
